Prompt

Oracle Events
OE 1: Search emails for the anonymous tip about the vendor behind the duplicate receipts. Discovers email from whistleblower8472@protonmail.com to dmitri.volkov@moveops.com with subject "RE: TechComfort Supply - not what you think", claiming TechComfort Supply is a fake vendor that operates a receipt generation website. The tipster mentions multiple template IDs (TC-RECEIPT-STD-041, 042, 043) that produce different hashes, and says someone at MoveOps shared the link internally. Tool: search_emails or list_emails.
OE 2: Search Airtable Stipend Transactions table for all TechComfort Supply submissions by searching the Review Notes field for "TechComfort". Discovers 7 total records: Kevin Tran (Vectral Systems, $89 keyboard, TC-STD-041, Approved), Andre Washington (Mosaic Robotics, $89 keyboard, TC-STD-041, Pending Approval), Jordan Ekwueme (BrightLoop Analytics, $45 monitor stand, TC-STD-041, Pending Approval), Jordan Ekwueme (BrightLoop Analytics, $127 desk lamp, TC-STD-043, Approved), Simone Richter (BrightLoop Analytics, $94 desk organizer, TC-STD-042, Approved), Ines Kovac (PivotPoint Labs, $78 keyboard tray, TC-STD-042, Approved), Noah Fitzgerald (GreenStack Energy, $112 monitor light bar, TC-STD-043, Approved). All 7 share identical /Creator metadata "TechComfort Supply Invoice Generator v2.1" despite using 3 different template IDs. Total exposure: $634 across 6 employees and 5 client accounts. Tool: airtable_list_bases, airtable_list_tables, airtable_search_records or airtable_list_records.
OE 3: Search Slack for mentions of "TechComfort" or "techcomfortsupply" to trace how the vendor link spread internally. Discovers Julian Brooks posted a vendor list in #general (channel C001) on Feb 12, 2026 that included the TechComfort link (techcomfortsupply.com/order), noting it has "a self-service receipt generator for bulk orders." He sourced it from Rachel Nguyen at Terraform. Tool: conversations_search_messages.
OE 4: Get thread replies to Julian's #general vendor list post to identify who distributed the link further. Discovers Emeka Diallo confirmed sharing the link with GreenStack and Mosaic employees, and Suki Patel confirmed forwarding it to Jordan at BrightLoop and PivotPoint employees. This explains how TechComfort receipts ended up across 5 client accounts. Tool: conversations_replies (channel_id: C001, thread_ts: 1770912000.000000).
OE 5: Search emails for "TechComfort" to find additional context about the expanded fraud scope. Discovers Hana Kim emailed Marcus Thorne (April 23) flagging TechComfort appearing on 7 stipend submissions across 5 accounts totaling $634. Marcus replied (April 24) CC'ing Dmitri, asking him to check forensic markers on the additional receipts and noting relaunch implications. Also discovers Emeka Diallo emailed Dmitri (April 24) expressing concern about having shared the TechComfort link. Tool: search_emails.
OE 6: Check the ENG-220 ticket (issue_stipend_duplicate_detection) and its comments to find Lena's Layer 2 documentation. Discovers the ticket is assigned to Dmitri, state: todo, under proj_expense_autocategorizer. Lena's comment (comment_lena_layer2_deferral, April 24) documents: Layer 1 (hash match) is DONE and deployed to staging, Layer 2 (PDF metadata similarity) is code complete in branch feature/eng-220-layer2 but NOT merged or deployed, Samira deferred Layer 2 to v2 to avoid delaying the Monday relaunch. Tool: linear_list_issues, linear_list_comments.
OE 7: List all issues under proj_expense_autocategorizer to verify the current state of every open ExpenseBot ticket. Discovers multiple tickets still in progress or todo: ENG-220 (state_todo), ENG-219 (state_todo), ENG-218 (state_in_progress), ENG-217 (state_in_progress), ENG-216 (state_in_progress), ENG-215 (state_in_progress), ENG-214 (state_in_progress), ENG-212 (state_in_progress), ENG-211 (state_in_progress). This contradicts Samira's claim that "everything is resolved." Tool: linear_list_issues.
OE 8: Search emails for Samira's relaunch communication to Elena. Discovers email from samira.tariq@moveops.com to elena.rostova@moveops.com (CC priya.chakrabarti@moveops.com), subject "ExpenseBot Relaunch - Ready for Monday April 28", dated April 25. Samira confirmed all critical remediation items complete, listed 5 completed items, noted Layer 2 deferred to v2, and recommended re-enabling auto-decisions Monday morning. Tool: search_emails.
OE 9: Search Slack #engineering for Samira's relaunch readiness announcement. Discovers Samira posted in #engineering (C003) on April 25: "ExpenseBot relaunch readiness check is GREEN. All P1 tickets are resolved or have workarounds in place." Listed ENG-214 through ENG-220 status and confirmed sending Elena the go-ahead email. Tool: conversations_search_messages.
OE 10: Search Slack #engineering for Dmitri's own pushback about Layer 2 and Samira's response. Discovers Dmitri posted (April 22) arguing that shipping without Layer 2 is a mistake and that the /Creator metadata is the smoking gun. Samira replied declining: "Elena has been very clear that she wants ExpenseBot back online Monday... Layer 2 goes into the next sprint." Tool: conversations_search_messages or conversations_replies.
OE 11: Add a forensic findings comment on ENG-220 (issue_stipend_duplicate_detection) covering all 7 TechComfort receipts, the /Creator metadata pattern across template IDs, affected employees and client accounts, total financial exposure ($634), how the link spread internally (Julian posted, Emeka and Suki distributed), and the implication for Layer 2 necessity. Tool: linear_create_comment.
OE 12: Create a new urgent Linear ticket assigned to Dmitri Volkov for the vendor fraud investigation as a separate workstream, with priority 0 (Urgent), covering the fraud scope, affected records, employees, client accounts, financial exposure, and internal link distribution chain. Tool: linear_create_issue.
OE 13: Post a message in #engineering (channel C003) informing the team about the potential systematic vendor fraud issue affecting the duplicate detection scope and that Dmitri is escalating to Samira and Marcus before any relaunch decision. Tool: conversations_add_message.
OE 14: Send an email from dmitri.volkov@moveops.com to samira.tariq@moveops.com, CC marcus.thorne@moveops.com, with the complete investigation findings including all 7 receipts, metadata pattern, financial exposure, how the link spread, why Layer 2 is now critical, and the request for Samira to reassess the Monday timeline. Tool: send_email (or reply_to_email).

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

14 The email to Samira explains the gap in Layer 1 detection, specifically that hash matching only catches identical files and would miss TechComfort receipts with different template IDs.
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The email states: 'Layer 1 (SHA-256 hash matching) would only catch 1 of these 7 receipts... Records #4–#7 all have unique hashes. Layer 1 would pass them through without any flag.' This clearly explains the hash-matching limitation.
✓Run #2The email explains: 'Layer 1 (SHA-256 hash match) catches ONLY receipts #1 and #2 — the identical file pair' and 'Layer 1 MISSES receipts #3 through #7 — different files, different hashes, but same fraudulent vendor.'
✓Run #3The email explicitly states: 'Layer 1 (hash matching) catches exactly ONE of the 7 fraudulent receipts — the Kevin Tran / Andre Washington duplicate pair. The other 5 receipts have unique hashes because they use different templates or parameters.'
✓Run #4The email explicitly explains: 'Layer 1 (SHA-256 hash matching) catches only the one exact-duplicate pair (#1 and #2). It will NOT catch receipts #4-7 because they have unique hashes despite being from the same fraudulent generator.'
✓Run #5The email explicitly explains: 'Layer 1 (SHA-256 hash matching) catches exactly 1 of these 7 fraudulent receipts (the Kevin/Andre identical file). The remaining 6 receipts use different templates and have different hashes — Layer 1 will not flag them'.
✓Run #6The email explicitly explains: 'Layer 1 (hash matching) catches only 1 out of 7 confirmed fraudulent receipts. The other 5 have unique hashes.' It explains that different template IDs produce different hashes and thus evade Layer 1 detection.

outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%

✓Run #3The email explicitly references Layer 2 (PDF /Creator metadata matching) as the solution that 'catches ALL 7' and explains the branch feature/eng-220-layer2 is code-complete.

✓Run #5The email explicitly states 'Layer 2 (PDF metadata similarity) would catch ALL 7 based on the shared /Creator string' and 'Lena's Layer 2 code is COMPLETE in branch feature/eng-220-layer2'.
✓Run #6The email explicitly states: 'Layer 2 (PDF metadata matching) catches ALL 7. The /Creator string is identical across every single one.' and 'Layer 2 code is COMPLETE in branch feature/eng-220-layer2.' It directly calls for Layer 2 to be included in the Monday release.
